Stub functions for handling ANCS-related BLE operations and analytics collection.

# Purpose
The code is a C source file that provides stub implementations for functions related to the Apple Notification Center Service (ANCS) in a Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) context. The file includes the header `ancs.h` and defines several functions that are intended to handle various ANCS-related operations, such as reading notifications, writing responses, performing actions on notifications, and managing service discovery and subscription. Each function is currently a stub, meaning it contains no operational code and simply returns without performing any actions.

The functions in this file are organized into two main sections: "Stub for PRF" and "Analytics." The "Stub for PRF" section includes functions like [`ancs_handle_read_or_notification`](<#ancs_handle_read_or_notification>), [`ancs_handle_write_response`](<#ancs_handle_write_response>), and [`ancs_perform_action`](<#ancs_perform_action>), which are placeholders for handling BLE characteristics and notifications. The "Analytics" section contains a single function, [`analytics_external_collect_ancs_info`](<#analytics_external_collect_ancs_info>), which is also a stub. This file is likely intended to be part of a larger system where these stubs will be replaced with actual implementations to interact with ANCS over BLE. The file does not define public APIs or external interfaces, as it primarily consists of internal function stubs.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `comm/ble/kernel_le_client/ancs/ancs.h`
